rejected_candidates.title: Swap return value is trusted without balance-delta validation
rejected_candidates.reason: Overlaps existing reserve-drain findings (F-001/F-005/F-009/F-010/F-018); as written it depends on a misbehaving swap router and does not add a distinct exploitable root cause.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing revert-message length guards can revert callback handlers
rejected_candidates.reason: Current protocol-generated revert messages are >=32 bytes; short-message failure requires atypical external misuse of these contracts as revert targets and does not show meaningful protocol-level exploitability.

rejected_candidates.title: `claimRefund` emits cleared values after deleting storage
rejected_candidates.reason: Informational event-quality issue only; no direct fund-loss, theft, lockup, or DoS impact.

rejected_candidates.title: Refund entry at `externalId == 0x0` is unclaimable
rejected_candidates.reason: Relies on non-standard zero externalId callback payloads; normal protocol paths derive non-zero IDs, so impact is largely self-inflicted and low practical risk.

rejected_candidates.title: GatewaySend.onCall returns incorrect success value causing callback failure
rejected_candidates.reason: Claim is based on an incorrect interface assumption and does not demonstrate an actual failing execution path in this codebase.

rejected_candidates.title: GatewaySend.onCall does not validate fromToken transfer quantity
rejected_candidates.reason: Already covered by existing amount/token trust and transfer-accounting findings (F-005/F-001).

rejected_candidates.title: GatewaySend.depositAndCall does not use swapData for output token calculation
rejected_candidates.reason: Duplicate of the existing asset-binding failure in F-010.

rejected_candidates.title: GatewaySend._doMixSwap missing validation for fromToken amount
rejected_candidates.reason: Duplicate of F-001 (attacker-controlled `fromTokenAmount` and token source mismatch).

rejected_candidates.title: GatewaySend depositAndCall lacks slippage protection for swap output
rejected_candidates.reason: Slippage constraints are user-specified in swap params (`minReturnAmount`); this is not a distinct protocol security bug.

rejected_candidates.title: GatewayTransferNative.onCall fee calculation uses input amount not output amount
rejected_candidates.reason: Economic/fee-policy concern, not a standalone exploit enabling theft, insolvency, lockup, or permissionless DoS.

rejected_candidates.title: GatewayTransferNative withdraw function is public not internal
rejected_candidates.reason: Already captured in accumulated finding F-008.

rejected_candidates.title: GatewayTransferNative.withdrawToNativeChain allows arbitrary recipient addresses without validation
rejected_candidates.reason: Receiver bytes are intentionally user-provided for multi-chain formats; invalid values are user-input risk and already covered where truncation causes loss (F-013/F-003).

rejected_candidates.title: GatewayCrossChain._swapAndSendERC20Tokens calculates amountInMax with slippage on quote not actual
rejected_candidates.reason: Standard exact-output quoting behavior; no distinct exploit beyond already captured allowance/residual and liveness issues.

rejected_candidates.title: GatewayCrossChain onCall does not validate message length before decoding
rejected_candidates.reason: Malformed payloads revert; no credible permissionless protocol-harm path beyond rejecting invalid input.

rejected_candidates.title: GatewayCrossChain claimRefund allows anyone to claim if walletAddress is 20 bytes and caller is bot
rejected_candidates.reason: Incorrect: for 20-byte wallet addresses payout goes to decoded receiver, not the bot caller.

rejected_candidates.title: SwapDataHelperLib.decodeCompressedMixSwapParams has unchecked arithmetic in offset calculations
rejected_candidates.reason: No practical overflow/memory-corruption path shown; out-of-bounds calldata slicing reverts safely under Solidity bounds checks.
